executiveSummary.summary: Strong technical execution capability but entering a commoditizing market with powerful incumbents. The AI screening space is rapidly becoming table stakes for existing ATS platforms. Trust barriers are real but surmountable. Success depends on finding a defensible niche beyond basic AI screening.

marketSize.tam: $3.2B global recruitment software market (Grand View Research 2023)
marketSize.sam: $800M mid-market ATS/recruiting software segment in US
marketSize.methodology: Based on ~50K mid-size companies in US, 10-20% adoption rate for standalone AI screening

competitors.name: HireVue
competitors.description: AI-powered video interviewing and assessment platform
competitors.strength: Established enterprise relationships, proven ROI metrics

competitors.name: Greenhouse
competitors.description: Leading ATS with AI-powered candidate scoring
competitors.strength: Full recruiting workflow, strong integrations, trusted brand
competitors.weakness: Higher price point, may over-engineer for simple screening needs

competitors.name: Workday HCM
competitors.description: Enterprise HR suite with AI recruiting features
competitors.strength: Complete HR ecosystem, enterprise sales force
competitors.weakness: Overkill for mid-market, slow to innovate

competitors.name: Lever
competitors.description: Modern ATS with built-in candidate intelligence
competitors.weakness: Still building AI features, limited customization

competitors.name: Pymetrics
competitors.description: AI-based talent matching and bias reduction
competitors.weakness: Complex setup, requires candidate buy-in for assessments

marketAnalysis.positioning: Simple, transparent AI screening for mid-market companies who want AI benefits without ATS complexity
marketAnalysis.timing: AI screening is becoming commoditized - window for standalone tools is narrowing rapidly

valueProposition.coreProblem: Mid-size companies waste 40+ hours per hire manually screening resumes, but full ATS platforms are overkill
valueProposition.targetCustomer: HR managers at 100-300 employee companies in high-volume hiring industries (tech, healthcare, retail)
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Plug-and-play AI screening that integrates with existing workflows, not a full ATS replacement
valueProposition.whyNow: Remote work increased application volumes 3x while AI screening accuracy crossed viability threshold in 2023

businessModel.revenueStrategy: SaaS subscription with usage-based tiers: $500/mo base (500 screenings), $0.50 per additional screening
businessModel.pricingFramework: $500/mo justified by saving 30 hours/month of manual screening (worth $900+ in HR time)
businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: At 10x scale (1000 customers), need dedicated customer success team and enterprise sales motion. Margins improve with scale due to fixed AI infrastructure costs.

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: FastAPI backend, React frontend, OpenAI/Claude API for NLP, PostgreSQL, deployed on Railway/Vercel

buildVsBuy.rationale: Textract or similar saves 4-6 weeks of development
Core differentiator, need control over prompts and logic
Simple CRUD app, faster to build than integrate

technicalRisks: AI API costs scaling faster than revenue
technicalFeasibility.estimatedMvpTimeline: 10-12 weeks for solo developer with your experience

risks.description: Established ATS players adding AI screening as a feature, making standalone tool obsolete
risks.mitigation: Focus on integration-first approach, partner with smaller ATS providers, or pivot to specialized verticals

risks.description: HR teams may not trust AI-only screening for legal/bias concerns
risks.mitigation: Build transparency features, partner with employment lawyers, get bias auditing certifications

risks.description: AI API costs could eat 40-60% of revenue at scale
risks.mitigation: Negotiate volume discounts early, consider fine-tuning smaller models, implement smart caching

risks.description: Increasing AI hiring regulations (NYC Local Law 144, EU AI Act)
risks.mitigation: Build audit trails, bias testing features, stay ahead of compliance requirements

risks.description: Solo founder burnout managing product, sales, and customer success
risks.mitigation: Hire part-time sales/marketing help by month 6, focus on product-led growth initially

week1to4: Interview 15 HR managers at target companies about current screening process
Research AI bias regulations in key markets
Set up basic landing page with email capture
week5to8: Create pricing and positioning based on customer feedback
week9to12: Build integrations with 2-3 popular HRIS systems

decisionAgenda.question: Should you build a standalone tool or pivot to an integration-first approach?

options.tradeoff: Gain: Full control, higher margins. Lose: Harder customer acquisition, competitive risk
Gain: Faster adoption, partner channels. Lose: Lower margins, dependency on partners

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start standalone but design API-first architecture. This lets you pivot to integrations if customer acquisition proves too expensive.

decisionAgenda.question: Which customer segment should you target first?

options.tradeoff: Gain: Large market. Lose: Generic positioning, harder to stand out
Gain: Easier to build reputation, specialized features. Lose: Smaller initial market

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Target tech companies 100-300 employees first. You understand their needs, they're comfortable with AI tools, and high hiring volumes justify the cost.

decisionAgenda.question: How much should you invest in bias detection and transparency features?

options.tradeoff: Gain: Faster MVP, lower costs. Lose: Harder enterprise sales, regulatory risk
Gain: Competitive differentiator, enterprise appeal. Lose: 4-6 weeks additional development

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with basic audit trails and explainable scoring. Add advanced bias detection in v2 based on customer demand.

decisionAgenda.question: Should you bootstrap or raise funding?

options.tradeoff: Gain: Full control, validation of business model. Lose: Slower growth, limited marketing budget
Gain: Faster growth, team building, marketing budget. Lose: Dilution, pressure, time spent fundraising

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Bootstrap to 20-30 paying customers first. This proves product-market fit and gives you leverage for better fundraising terms.
